message:
chore(beads): close ft-ljgyr + ft-h90rh, file ft-hg6io

ft-ljgyr (E0599 compile blocker) fixed in 810ad4c9 — swarm unblocked.
ft-h90rh storage infra shipped in 4595cc0e. Wiring into
mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation + policy.rs helper + round-trip test filed
as ft-hg6io (blocked on mcp_tools.rs reservation + a clean policy.rs).

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -196,10 +196,11 @@
 {"id":"ft-h7kwb","title":"[mock] runtime_compat.rs:1334 tokio::select\! still re-exported","description":"runtime_compat.rs:1334 has `pub use tokio::select;` with TODO comment: "replace with asupersync::select\! or futures::select\! once available to fully decouple from tokio." This is the last remaining tokio dependency after ft-xbnl0.2.5 collapsed the dual-runtime feature seam. Production behavior should be: replace with asupersync::select\! (if/when available) or futures::select\! to fully eliminate the tokio crate dependency. Currently functions correctly — tokio::select\! is a poll combinator that works without a live tokio runtime — so this is tech debt, not a bug.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:35.365188Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:47:03.507349Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:47:03.507032Z","close_reason":"done — replaced pub use tokio::select with custom macro_rules! select using futures::future::select + pin_mut! (f58ed8f1)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-h8da2","title":"[security][P3] Verify Redactor is applied on every pane-content read path (design-audit)","description":"## Context

Pane content is stored UNREDACTED at write time: crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:12363 append_segment_sync inserts the raw content string directly into output_segments.content with no Redactor invocation, while peer write paths (set_event_note_sync at :12603 redacts; record_audit_action_redacted at :6647 redacts; undo metadata at :6701 redacts) DO call Redactor::new().redact(...).

This is a deliberate design choice: preserve full fidelity at rest, apply redaction at the serving boundary (get-text, search, event emission, export, audit, web/** handlers, MCP tool envelopes). The README calls this out: 'Captured output is redacted before being returned through any API surface.'

The design is sound but fragile: every read path must redact. A missing redaction on even ONE read path leaks secrets (API keys, session tokens, agent-prompt credentials) that an operator reasonably assumed were filtered.

## Scope of this task

NOT a code change. A deliberate audit of every pane-content read path in the repo, producing a short matrix:

| Read path | File:line | Redacts? |
|-----------|-----------|----------|

Paths to check (non-exhaustive):
- wa.get_text MCP tool (mcp_tools.rs)
- wa.search MCP tool (mcp_tools.rs)
- ft robot get-text CLI (main.rs)
- ft robot search CLI (main.rs)
- /panes /events /search /stream/* web handlers (web/handlers.rs, web/sse.rs)
- ft export (export.rs)
- ft audit / ft history (storage access paths)
- ft replay (replay.rs decoded frame payloads — FrameType::Output content flows back out verbatim)
- Distributed aggregator forwarding (distributed.rs)

Each row of the matrix should cite the specific line that does or does NOT invoke Redactor::redact (or equivalent).

## Why P3

Not a known vulnerability — no specific read path is known to skip redaction. But the fragile invariant + large surface means a quiet leak is the most plausible way secrets end up exposed. Cheap to verify; one-time cost.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Matrix committed under docs/security/ with every pane-content read path enumerated.
- Any path missing redaction becomes its own follow-up bead (per-path P1 at that point).
- If no gaps found, matrix serves as a regression benchmark future refactors can diff against.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow during /security-audit-for-saas pass 2 (storage.rs + replay.rs + ingest.rs), 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:22:07.728866Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:29:11.463264Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:29:11.462771Z","close_reason":"Audit matrix landed in 7a878654 at docs/security/read-path-redaction-matrix.md. Every outbound surface (MCP, robot CLI, web API, export/replay/audit) enumerated with file:line evidence for each Redactor invocation. One concrete LEAK found: wa.state MCP tool + ft robot state CLI return pane title/cwd unredacted, while the web /panes sibling redacts them. Filed ft-yj375 (P1) with a 1-line fix path. Known deferred: distributed.rs aggregator forwarding — zero Redactor references, feature-gated off by default, full audit called out as future work in the matrix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","redaction","security","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-h8u5y","title":"[gap] ft-xbnl0 completion evidence cargo-check pass not matched by current compile blockers","description":"docs/ft-xbnl0-5-7-completion-evidence.md:18-27 claims `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-jemanuel-target cargo check -p frankenterm` passed remotely; rerunning on April 21, 2026 with `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-codex-reality-target cargo check -p frankenterm` now fails with compile blockers at crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/sse.rs:231, crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:3433-3437, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:1921-2019.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:24.027291Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.985833Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:16:04.289515Z","close_reason":"Verified workspace cargo check still FAILS on 2026-04-21, but blockers have rotated since the bead was filed:

Original bead citations (from code at time of filing):
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/sse.rs:231
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:3433-3437
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:1921-2019

Reinspection today: those three specific line ranges now contain clean code (closing brace/impl header at sse.rs:231, normal debug! logging at runtime.rs:3433, normal loop body at mux_client.rs:1921). The original blockers appear resolved by the intervening 9e1bd63b cfg-strip + c1fb747e mux_client delimiter fix cascade.

Current blockers (via rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target cargo check --workspace --all-targets):
1. frankenterm/term/tests/proptest_misc_serde.rs:83 — 17-tuple exceeds proptest::strategy::Strategy impl (same class as proptest_pane_serde seen during ft-wajba verification).
2. crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/sse.rs:601-608 — select! macro fallout under --all-targets; 13 errors in frankenterm-core lib when built with all targets. The macro pattern was likely touched by 9e1bd63b's runtime_compat cfg-strip and left in an inconsistent shape.

The claim in docs/ft-xbnl0-5-7-completion-evidence.md:18-27 ('cargo check -p frankenterm passed remotely') remains STALE against workspace-wide check. However the scope of the gap has narrowed substantially: compile failures are now in test/proptest surfaces and one remaining select! macro site, not in the three runtime surfaces the bead originally cited.

Closing this bead as 'verified stale but blockers rotated'. A fresh fix bead ft-h9g0q has been filed covering the current blockers (web/sse.rs select! + proptest 17-tuple) so a future triage pass picks up the actual current compile-state gap rather than chasing the stale line numbers in this bead's body.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
-{"id":"ft-h90rh","title":"[security][follow-up ft-6mmyp] Persist policy-denied MCP mutations to audit_actions table (not just tracing log)","description":"## Context

ft-6mmyp primary fix shipped: mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation now emits structured tracing::warn! on every deny / require_approval under target ft::security::policy with tool / command / decision / rule_id / reason fields. That closes the 'zero observability' gap for denied MCP mutations.

The bead's ideal (fuller) fix was a record_audit_action_redacted write so denied attempts appear in the same audit_actions table as successful ones — enables SQL-queryable forensics and parity with the wa.events_annotate post-mutation audit at mcp_tools.rs:4330-4352.

## Why deferred

record_audit_action_redacted is async. The current sync gate helper at mcp_tools.rs:177-231 can't await. The callers (tx_run/rollback, mission_pause/resume/abort) all do already live inside a runtime.block_on scope, but the helper is invoked BEFORE that block_on — specifically to fail fast without opening a StorageHandle. Opening storage inside the helper on the deny path would:
  - Open a new StorageHandle (~100ms cold path)
  - Require passing db_path (currently the helper only takes &Config)
  - Add a runtime.block_on inside a sync fn that may already be inside a tokio runtime → panic risk

## Fix path

Either:
(a) Push the audit write into the deny branch of each of the 5 caller handlers (they already have runtime.block_on scopes) — duplicates ~10 lines per caller but avoids helper signature changes.
(b) Refactor mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation to return a deny-decision value and have the caller write the audit row plus the error envelope — single point of responsibility per tool.
(c) Change the helper signature to async fn mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation(config, db_path, ...) -> ... and have callers await it inside their existing runtime.block_on.

(b) is cleanest. Roughly:

    enum GateOutcome {
        Allow,
        Deny { envelope: McpResult<Vec<Content>>, audit: AuditActionRecord },
        RequireApproval { envelope: McpResult<Vec<Content>>, audit: AuditActionRecord },
    }
    fn mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation(config, summary, command_text, start) -> GateOutcome

Callers match, write the audit via their existing storage in the deny/approval branches, then return the envelope.

## Acceptance Criteria

- After deny/approval, audit_actions has a row with result='denied' or 'require_approval', decision_reason=policy_reason, action_kind matching the tool.
- Integration test per tool: seed a deny-config, call tool, assert audit row shape.
- Tracing log emission (shipped under ft-6mmyp main fix) remains in place as a second signal.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing the ft-6mmyp MVP, 2026-04-23.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:49.814251Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:36:05.696029Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","mcp","policy","security"]}

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing the ft-6mmyp MVP, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:49.814251Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:54.335685Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:54.333554Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4595cc0e: storage.rs-side infrastructure for persistent policy-denial audit. Added: SCHEMA_VERSION bump 23→24, idempotent migration creating policy_denied_audit table (ts_ms/agent_id/tool_name/intent_hash/reason/reason_code/rule_id/decision + two indices), PolicyDeniedAuditRecord struct with stable reason_code + decision const, WriteCommand::RecordPolicyDenialAudit variant (+ label arm), record_policy_denial_audit_sync writer path, and StorageHandle::record_policy_denial_audit + cx-first sibling. Down migration is a clean DROP. Deliberately out of scope: policy.rs convenience wrapper + mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation wiring — pane 2 had a +81/-16 uncommitted diff on policy.rs (ft-eu0no shared-rate-limiter work) so I left it untouched to avoid collision. Filing ft-<new> as the wiring bead that needs both mcp_tools.rs scope and a clean-enough policy.rs; wiring test (round-trip insert+select) belongs there too.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","mcp","policy","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-h9g0q","title":"Workspace cargo check fails: web/sse.rs select! macro + frankenterm-term proptest 17-tuple","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:03:57.002490Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:04.379090Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:04.378252Z","close_reason":"Sweep landed across two commits: 87d4546b (error_renderer + retry non-exhaustive match after Error-variant additions) and 0b65bc0b (which absorbed my staged patches to proptest_input_serde, proptest_color_serde, proptest_keycode_serde, bintree Clone derive, and the mux 17-tuple split). Workspace check advances past the five errors the bead named. Remaining codec::proptest_codec_roundtrip 67-error block is separate scope — drifted test expectations against post-rename Pdu types, not a build-system failure.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3603,"issue_id":"ft-h9g0q","author":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","text":"Shipped mechanical fix for the error_renderer.rs:34 + retry.rs:378 non-exhaustive match sites in commit 87d4546b. Strategy: route all three new variants (RuntimeOperation, PaneOperation, WatchdogWarningRead) through the existing generic buckets (FT-9001 code, is_retryable=true) with comments at each site citing this bead. Trivially supersedable if you want per-variant error codes / source-driven retry decisions — the code comments point at your refactor as the proper home for that. Separately surfaced in my scan pass: the select! macro error at web/sse.rs:601 referenced in the title isn't visible in --no-default-features --features asupersync-runtime builds; might be a --workspace / --all-targets-only surface. Not touched here.","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-ha67e","title":"pattern pack sandbox swallows non-dangling canonicalize failures","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:59:18.467501Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:36.519825Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:36.519051Z","close_reason":"Landed in 6a8e698d (fix(patterns): sandbox_resolve_dir propagates canonicalize errors except NotFound)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-he0w7","title":"[fuzz] add native_events::decode_wire_event fuzz target for socket wire protocol","description":"Surface: crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs:521 — fn decode_wire_event(line: &str) -> Result<Option<NativeEvent>, String>. Parses newline-delimited JSON wire events from the wezterm event socket (WireEvent::{Hello,PaneOutput,StateChange,UserVar,PaneCreated,...}). The PaneOutput variant embeds a base64-encoded data_b64 payload that becomes a Vec<u8> bounded by MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES. No existing fuzz target covers this protocol boundary.

Proposed harness: fuzz/fuzz_targets/native_events_wire.rs
  #![no_main]
  use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
  fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
      if data.len() > 64_000 { return; }
      let Ok(line) = std::str::from_utf8(data) else { return; };
      // decode_wire_event is private. Expose via pub(crate) or a
      // test-only re-export, OR target the nearest public entry point
      // (event_stream::try_decode_line if it exists, otherwise add
      //  gated behind a 
      // feature).
      let _ = frankenterm_core::native_events::decode_wire_event_for_fuzz(line);
  });

Corpus seeds: fuzz/corpus/native_events_wire/ — one file per WireEvent variant with realistic ts/pane_id values, plus malformed-base64 PaneOutput, oversized data_b64 (>MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES to hit truncation), empty line, non-UTF-8-in-JSON-string, deeply-nested escape sequences.

Changes required: add pub(crate) fn decode_wire_event_for_fuzz in native_events.rs behind #[cfg(any(test, feature = "fuzz"))].","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:18.642190Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:19:00.447094Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:19:00.446086Z","close_reason":"Fixed in ea93a349","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-hg6io","title":"[security][follow-up ft-h90rh] Wire mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation into record_policy_denial_audit (call-site + policy.rs helper + test)","description":"## Context

ft-h90rh storage-side infra shipped in 4595cc0e: policy_denied_audit table + StorageHandle::record_policy_denial_audit async method are live. What's still missing to close the observability loop end-to-end:

1. A convenience wrapper in crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs (e.g. `fn record_policy_denial(storage: &StorageHandle, decision: &PolicyDecision, tool_name: &str, agent_id: Option<&str>, intent_hash: Option<&str>) -> Result<()>`) that builds the PolicyDeniedAuditRecord from a PolicyDecision and writes it. Skipped in the infra ship because policy.rs had an active +81/-16 uncommitted diff from pane 2's ft-eu0no rate-limiter refactor.
2. Wire the wrapper into crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs::mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation alongside the existing tracing::warn!. Challenge: the gate helper is sync; record_policy_denial_audit is async. Options:
   - (a) Gate helper returns a GateOutcome enum carrying the PolicyDeniedAuditRecord; each of the 5 callers (tx_run/rollback, mission_pause/resume/abort) — which already live inside runtime.block_on scopes — writes the row in its deny branch.
   - (b) Fire-and-forget spawn inside the sync gate via runtime_compat::spawn + block_on(None) pattern, dropping the Result.
   (a) is cleaner and per-caller controllable; (b) is smaller diff but loses errors.
3. A round-trip integration test: open a fresh DB, run migration, call record_policy_denial_audit with a known record, query it back, assert equality. Bonus: a per-tool deny-policy end-to-end test that exercises the full mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation → audit-row pipeline.

## Acceptance Criteria

- A denied wa.tx_run (or any of the 5 gated tools) under a deny policy produces both a tracing::warn! emission AND a row in policy_denied_audit queryable via SELECT.
- Integration test covering the round-trip.
- policy.rs helper has at least one unit test that builds a record from a synthetic PolicyDecision.

## Scope note

Requires mcp_tools.rs reservation and a clean-enough policy.rs to avoid collision with ft-eu0no. Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing ft-h90rh infra, 2026-04-23.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:43:12.398456Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:43:12.398456Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","follow-up","mcp","policy","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-hsmjb","title":"Event::PaneDisappeared declared but never emitted from production runtime","description":"events.rs:181 declared Event::PaneDisappeared. event_stream filters, wire_protocol serializer, main.rs Web SSE route, and tests all matched on it — but no production code path in runtime.rs::handle_native_event ever called bus.publish(PaneDisappeared). Consumers remained dormant forever. Fix landed in 1f3ae436: publish on NativeEvent::PaneDestroyed guarded on event_bus.is_some(). Tests landed in 41cab3bb: ft_pp7jk_pane_destroyed_publishes_pane_disappeared_event + companion no-bus path. Symmetrical to Event::PaneDiscovered which was already published. Unlocks future long-lived subsystems (policy caches, connector bridges) that want per-pane state teardown.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:08:33.083454Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:24.250654Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:24.250393Z","close_reason":"Landed in 1f3ae436 (fix(runtime): publish Event::PaneDisappeared from handle_native_event) + 41cab3bb (tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-htf0i","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:57:00.591671Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.138080Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.137486Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-hyrav","title":"events::NotificationCooldown::check: insertion_order not refreshed on cooldown-expired send, LRU evicts actively-used keys","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:1015-1064 NotificationCooldown::check has a subtle LRU-staleness bug: when a key's cooldown has EXPIRED and we emit Send (line 1036-1043), we update entry.last_notified to now and reset entry.suppressed_since_notify, but we do NOT move the key to the back of self.insertion_order. The key stays at its ORIGINAL insertion position. Next time the entries map hits max_capacity, the eviction path at line 1047-1050 pops insertion_order.pop_front() — which picks a key by ORIGINAL INSERTION ORDER, not by last-use. A busy key that's been 'refreshed' (send + reset) many times can be evicted while a truly-dormant key with a later insertion stays. Effect: the cooldown state is silently dropped for the recently-used key; subsequent check() sees no entry and treats it as a first occurrence, flooding out a notification that should have been suppressed or at least carried a 'suppressed_since_last' count. Same pattern already fixed in sibling EventDeduplicator at events.rs:867-870 (refresh insertion_order on window-expired reset). Fix: in the cooldown-expired Send branch, remove the key from its current insertion_order position and push_back — matching the EventDeduplicator pattern. Regression test: 3-capacity cooldown, insert A/B/C, advance time past cooldown + re-check A (causing expired Send + refresh), insert D (triggering eviction). Pre-fix A is evicted; post-fix B is evicted (truly oldest-by-use).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:24.607054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:34:50.222010Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:34:50.221646Z","close_reason":"Landed in 36c6f7d3 (fix(events): refresh NotificationCooldown insertion_order on cooldown-expired send). Mirrors EventDeduplicator's window-expired refresh pattern at events.rs:867-870. Regression test cooldown_expired_send_refreshes_lru_position_ft_hyrav pins the post-fix eviction order. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -226,7 +227,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-lekgj","title":"[MEDIUM] HeadlessMuxServer::Heartbeat silently ACKs unknown peers, producing split-brain with pruned federation state","description":"# Defect

## File + line

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/headless_mux_server.rs:423` — `HeadlessMuxServer::handle_request`, `RemoteRequest::Heartbeat` arm.

## Concrete failure path

```rust
RemoteRequest::Heartbeat { from, pane_count } => {
    if let Some(peer) = self.peers.get_mut(&from.node_id) {
        peer.last_heartbeat_at = epoch_ms();
        peer.pane_count = pane_count;
        peer.status = PeerStatus::Connected;
    }
    RemoteResponse::HeartbeatAck
}
```

**If the heartbeat arrives from a node that is NOT in `self.peers`, the handler silently drops it and still returns `HeartbeatAck`.** This is reachable in two natural situations:

1. **Post-prune race.** Peer N missed enough heartbeats to be marked `Unreachable` by `check_peer_health()`, then removed by `prune_unreachable_peers()`. The peer is still running, its network partition heals, and it sends its next heartbeat. Server has no record of N, silently ACKs, peer thinks federation is healthy, server thinks it has fewer peers. Classic split-brain.
2. **Heartbeat before JoinFederation.** A misconfigured or restarted peer starts heartbeating before its `JoinFederation` roundtrip completes (race on a lossy network where JoinFederation gets reordered behind the first Heartbeat). Server silently ACKs. Peer sees success. Server keeps acting without the peer in its registry.

In both cases:
- `ListPeers` on the server returns a stale list (missing the real peer).
- `federated_pane_count` undercounts — pane_count from the unknown peer is thrown away.
- No telemetry, no warn log.

## Severity rationale

- Silent wrong answer (split-brain between peer's belief and server's registry), affecting federation telemetry and potentially workload scheduling if `federated_pane_count` feeds a placement decision.
- Not a crash, not data corruption.
- Recovery requires the peer to notice it's not in ListPeers and re-`JoinFederation` — the peer has no prompt to do that today because HeartbeatAck looks successful.

## Fix sketch

Return a specific error when the peer is unknown so the sender can re-JoinFederation and recover automatically:

```rust
RemoteRequest::Heartbeat { from, pane_count } => {
    if let Some(peer) = self.peers.get_mut(&from.node_id) {
        peer.last_heartbeat_at = epoch_ms();
        peer.pane_count = pane_count;
        peer.status = PeerStatus::Connected;
        RemoteResponse::HeartbeatAck
    } else {
        RemoteResponse::Error {
            code: "peer_not_federated".into(),
            message: format!("node {} has not joined this server; re-send JoinFederation", from.node_id),
        }
    }
}
```

Clients receiving `peer_not_federated` should re-send `JoinFederation`. The error code is stable so clients can pattern-match.

## Tests

* `ft_lekgj_heartbeat_for_unknown_peer_returns_error` — Server never saw `JoinFederation`, Heartbeat returns `Error { code: peer_not_federated, .. }` instead of `HeartbeatAck`.
* `ft_lekgj_heartbeat_after_prune_returns_error` — Peer joined, went unreachable, got pruned, then sent a fresh heartbeat; same error surface so the peer knows to rejoin.
* `ft_lekgj_heartbeat_for_known_peer_still_acks` — Regression: the happy path still yields `HeartbeatAck` and updates last_heartbeat_at/pane_count/status.

## Scope

Single file (`headless_mux_server.rs`). No public-API break — `RemoteResponse::Error` already exists and is already used for other failure modes in the same match.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:38:51.321265Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:42:27.477333Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:42:27.477025Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-lnlg4","title":"[reality-gap] tui/ftui_stub.rs named _stub but fully implemented (8296 LOC, header: 'no placeholders remain')","description":"# Code reality

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_stub.rs`:
  - 8296 lines (non-trivial)
  - Header comment line 14: "All view bodies (Home, Panes, Search,
    Help, Events, Triage, History, Timeline) are backed by live
    state — no placeholders remain."
  - `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/mod.rs:103` declares
    `mod ftui_stub;` and re-exports `App, AppConfig, View,
    ViewState, run_tui` from it.

So the module name says "stub" but the header and the usage prove
it's the production FrankenTUI backend.

# Why this is a reality-check gap

Every sweep for placeholders/unimplemented code in this crate (this
one included) flags `ftui_stub` as a candidate for further
investigation, then discovers it's a false positive. Reality-check
sweeps burn cycles on this; code-review agents lose focus on real
gaps.

The naming is a legacy from when the file genuinely was a stub. The
content has grown into the real backend. The file name didn't
follow.

# Recommended disposition

```bash
git mv crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_stub.rs \
       crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_backend.rs
```

…then update the one `mod ftui_stub` declaration and the two
`super::ftui_stub::` re-export lines. 15-second edit, preserves
git history via `git mv`.

# Risk

- `git mv` preserves blame + history.
- No external dependency on the file name (it's a sibling module).
- Existing grep-based tooling that searches for "ftui_stub" would
  miss it post-rename — worth a global grep + a deprecation comment
  left at `mod.rs` during the rename window.

# Severity

Lowest of the 5 reality-check gaps filed this pass (ft-z01cl,
ft-trby3, ft-6269x, ft-x4k2b are all higher-impact), but the
cheapest to fix. Good first-issue material.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:45:46.543886Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:55.904251Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:54:09.846972Z","close_reason":"Verified in a68246b7 (fix(tui): wire ftui_backend module rename follow-through) + 8c8f505b which completed the git mv ftui_stub.rs → ftui_backend.rs and updated mod.rs/keymap.rs/rollout.rs references.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hygiene","reality-gap","tui"]}
 {"id":"ft-lokam","title":"[LOW] diag bundle environment.json leaks absolute workspace cwd; tracing fields leak operator paths","description":"Found during /security-audit-for-saas pass #6 (pane 5, 2026-04-21).

## Summary
Three logging/telemetry surfaces all come out clean on the obvious
secret-leak vectors (no bearer-token / API-key / pane-content
fields in tracing calls; no /etc/ or .ssh/ reads in diag
collection). One defense-in-depth gap remains: path privacy.

## Findings

### F1 [LOW] diag bundle environment.json includes absolute cwd
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/diagnostic.rs:87-89
  ```rust
  cwd: std::env::current_dir()
      .ok()
      .map(|p| p.display().to_string()),
  ```
- Diag bundles (`ft diag bundle`) are often attached to bug reports
  and shared outside the host. An absolute cwd reveals:
  - the user's home path segment (`/Users/<username>/...` or
    `/home/<username>/...`),
  - the project path (`/Users/alice/projects/secret-startup-pitch-deck/`),
  - on some setups, mount points that hint at filesystem layout.
- Severity: LOW. Users implicitly share the workspace when
  debugging, but the current behavior removes the choice.

### F2 [LOW] tracing fields log operator-private paths
- Multiple `tracing::[info|warn|debug]!(..., path = %X.display(), ...)`
  call sites scatter socket paths, DB paths, and workspace paths
  through structured logs. Representative hits (non-exhaustive):
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs:258, 302, 331,
      362, 401 — unix socket paths.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs:1118 —
      `info_span!("telemetry_store_open", path = %db_path.display())`.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:1375, 2658, 2814 —
      socket paths on accept failures.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs:265 —
      `meta_path = %self.meta_path.display()`.
- Severity: LOW. These logs land in the user's local log file,
  not network-visible by default, but they do end up in diag
  bundles when `collect_recent_log_lines` is called.

## Non-findings verified in the same pass
- Bearer tokens in structured logs: grep across
  `tracing::{info,debug,warn,error}!` call sites for
  `token|bearer|secret|password|api_key` returned zero production
  hits. Clean.
- Pane content in tracing fields: no production code logs
  `segment.content`, `delta_text`, or `pane_text`. Only test
  `assert_eq!(segment.content, ...)` references found. Clean.
- Diag bundle reading privileged files: no `/etc/`, `.ssh/`,
  `.aws/`, `.gnupg/` reads in `diagnostic.rs`. The bundle only
  reads the ft SQLite DB (already redacted via `redact_events`)
  and writes to the workspace-local `diag_dir`. Clean.
- Environment variables in diag bundle: `gather_environment`
  captures only OS / arch / rust_version / wa_version / cwd —
  no `std::env::vars()` dump. Clean.
- Summarize_config: the docstring already calls out "no paths or
  secrets" and the implementation confirms — only numeric/boolean
  config values are copied over. Clean.

## Fix direction
- F1: replace absolute cwd with just the basename, or gate
  inclusion behind `DiagnosticOptions.include_cwd: bool` default
  `false`. Concretely at diagnostic.rs:87:
  ```rust
  cwd: if opts.include_cwd {
      std::env::current_dir().ok().map(|p| p.display().to_string())
  } else {
      std::env::current_dir()
          .ok()
          .and_then(|p| p.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned()))
  },
  ```
- F2: when a log line is included in a diag bundle via
  `collect_recent_log_lines` (or equivalent), run it through the
  existing `Redactor` path-aware rules. Non-bundle in-process logs
  stay as-is since they aid operator debugging on the local box.

## Severity rationale
- LOW (P2): neither finding reveals a secret, and both sit behind
  an operator action (`ft diag bundle` + sharing it). The fix
  shrinks the unintentional-share footprint but doesn't close a
  live exploit.

## Related
- ft-ybtyg (598a7c13) — snapshot/checkpoint hashing honesty.
- ft-2guux (doc reality-check) — complementary; this bead is the
  diagnostics-exfil counterpart.

Filed per /security-audit-for-saas pass #6.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:48:38.534325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:53:28.901879Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:53:28.901341Z","close_reason":"Closed by e790f481. F1 (cwd leak) fixed: DiagnosticOptions gains include_full_cwd:bool (default false). gather_environment now routes through new scrub_path_to_basename helper which returns only the final path component; opt-in via include_full_cwd:true restores absolute path for legitimate mount-point/multi-host repro cases. Both production entry points (generate_bundle + generate_bundle_with_cx) forward opts. 8 regression tests cover basename-only default, root/empty fallback to <scrubbed> sentinel, relative paths, opt-in restore, and the strongest guarantee: default environment.json output cannot contain /Users/ or /home/ segments. F2 (tracing field path-leak via collect_recent_log_lines) deferred as follow-up — requires routing log-line bundling through the existing Redactor infrastructure, out of scope for the 25-min cap but documented inline in the commit body for the next pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","diagnostics","logging","privacy","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-ltpog","title":"native_events: decode errors logged at debug, invisible in production","description":"# [MEDIUM] native_events: malformed wire events logged at debug

**Subsystem:** native_events / protocol_recovery
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs (`handle_connection_with_cx`)

## The hole

When wire-event decoding fails (invalid JSON, bad base64, unknown event
type), the handler emitted:

\`\`\`rust
Err(err) => {
    debug!(error = %err, "failed to decode native event (cx path)");
}
\`\`\`

A malformed wire event is a protocol anomaly — version skew between
frankenterm and the mux-server, on-wire corruption, or a hostile peer
writing into the native-events socket. Operators routinely filter
`debug!` in production, so the signal was effectively lost.

Contrast with the `MAX_EVENT_LINE_BYTES` path a few lines above, which
already emits at `warn!`. Same class of anomaly, inconsistent level.

## Fix

Promoted the decode-error log to `warn!`. The `warn!` import was already
present — no dependency change.

Future work (separate bead): attach a telemetry counter for
decode-failure rate so it can drive a SLI rather than relying solely on
log sampling.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:39.950825Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:46:57.568904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:46:57.568595Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["native-events","observability"]}
